translation: By the emissaries [winds], recognized by feel [blowing gently],
notes: The emissaries are the winds which man cannot see but can recognize only when he does feel them blowing gently against his body.

translation: Then by the storms blowing violently,
By those [winds] that scatter [clouds] far and wide,
Then by those [winds] that make an uneven distribution [of rain-bearing clouds],
Then by those [winds] that bring down what is often talked of [rains]
As excuse for or harbinger of calamity,
notes: Lack of rains would be an excuse (cause) for drought conditions, and, heavy rains, a harbinger of destructive floods.

translation: What you are promised [Day of Resurrection/Judgment] is indeed inevitable.
So when the stars are extinguished,
And when the sky is torn asunder,
And when the mountains are brought down,
And when the Messengers are presented at the time appointed –
For what day is the time fixed?
For the Day of Decision.
And what do you understand what the Day of Decision is?
Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!
Did We not destroy the earlier generations?
And then caused the later generations to follow them?
Thus do We deal with the sinners!
Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!
Did We not create you from a filthy fluid?
And then placed it [fertilized ovum] in a safe abode [womb]
For a known period [nine months and some days, normally]?
We then fixed terms [for everything in man’s life]. And We are the best in fixing terms!
Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!
Have We not made the earth, accommodating
The living and the dead?

arabic: وَجَعَلۡنَا فِيهَا رَوَٰسِيَ شَٰمِخَٰتٖ وَأَسۡقَيۡنَٰكُم مَّآءٗ فُرَاتٗا
transliteration: WajaAAalna feeha rawasiya shamikhatin waasqaynakum maan furatan
translation: And [have We not] placed therein lofty mountains and given you sweet water to drink?
Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!

arabic: ٱنطَلِقُوٓاْ إِلَىٰ مَا كُنتُم بِهِۦ تُكَذِّبُونَ
transliteration: Intaliqoo ila ma kuntum bihi tukaththiboona
translation: “Proceed now to what (Hereafter) you had been denying!”

arabic: ٱنطَلِقُوٓاْ إِلَىٰ ظِلّٖ ذِي ثَلَٰثِ شُعَبٖ
transliteration: Intaliqoo ila thillin thee thalathi shuAAabin
translation: “Proceed now to the three-tier shadow [– of death, Resurrection, and divine Judgment]!”

arabic: لَّا ظَلِيلٖ وَلَا يُغۡنِي مِنَ ٱللَّهَبِ
transliteration: La thaleelin wala yughnee mina allahabi
translation: (The shadow provides) no relief nor shelter from the Blazing Fire [of Hell].
It does indeed throw up sparks like burning logs of fire-wood,
[Or] like thick yellow ropes.
Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!
This Day they speak not,
Nor are they permitted to put up excuses.
Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!
This is the Day of Decision. We have brought you and the people living in the world earlier, together.
If now you have any scheme, dare to use it against Me!
Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!
Pious persons are amidst shades and fountains,
And [they get] fruits such as they desire.

arabic: كُلُواْ وَٱشۡرَبُواْ هَنِيٓـَٔۢا بِمَا كُنتُمۡ تَعۡمَلُونَ
transliteration: Kuloo waishraboo haneean bima kuntum taAAmaloona
translation: “Eat and drink to your satisfaction as a reward for your [good] deeds.”
Thus indeed do We reward the good!
Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!
Eat and enjoy but little. You are sinners indeed!
Woe that Day [Hereafter] for those who had denied the Truth!

arabic: وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱرۡكَعُواْ لَا يَرۡكَعُونَ
transliteration: Waitha qeela lahumu irkaAAoo la yarkaAAoona
translation: And when they are told to bow down, they bow not down!
notes: See [68:42] and study note 2 thereunder. In that Verse, we are told that the sinners would not be able to prostrate. They would not be able to bow too, as per this Verse here.

translation: Woe that Day for those who had denied the Truth!
What hadeeth would they then believe in after this?
notes: Refer study note 74 on [7:185] in this context.
